---
applyTo: '**/Module.cpp,**/Module.h'
---

# 10.1 — Plugin Module Files

> Part of the [10 — Plugin Development](10-plugin-development.md) series.

---

## Summary

1. [Module Name Convention](#module-name-convention)
2. [Module.h Structure](#moduleh-structure)
3. [Module.cpp Structure](#modulecpp-structure)

---

## Module Name Convention

### Requirement

- Every plugin **must** define `MODULE_NAME` because Thunder uses it to uniquely identify the plugin at runtime.
- `MODULE_NAME` must always carry the prefix `Plugin_` followed by the plugin name in PascalCase.
- The definition must be placed inside an `#ifndef` / `#endif` guard so it can be overridden from the build system if needed.

### Example

In `Module.h`:

```cpp
#ifndef MODULE_NAME
#define MODULE_NAME Plugin_NetworkControl
#endif
```

---

## Module.h Structure

### Requirement

- `Module.h` must use `#pragma once` (not a legacy `#ifndef` header guard).
- It must define `MODULE_NAME` **before** including any Thunder headers, because those headers reference the macro.
- Include only the core Thunder plugin headers needed by every translation unit in the plugin.
- Re-define `EXTERNAL` to an empty macro after the includes so the plugin does not accidentally export symbols that should remain internal.
- `Module.h` must be the **first** include in every `.cpp` file in the plugin.

### Example

```cpp
#pragma once

#ifndef MODULE_NAME
#define MODULE_NAME Plugin_NetworkControl
#endif

#include <plugins/plugins.h>
#include <definitions/definitions.h>

#undef EXTERNAL
#define EXTERNAL
```

Additional plugin-specific headers (cryptography helpers, RPC definitions, etc.) may be added after the core includes:

```cpp
#pragma once

#ifndef MODULE_NAME
#define MODULE_NAME Plugin_BluetoothControl
#endif

#include <bluetooth/bluetooth.h>
#include <plugins/plugins.h>
#include <definitions/definitions.h>

#undef EXTERNAL
#define EXTERNAL
```

---

## Module.cpp Structure

### Requirement

- `Module.cpp` must include `"Module.h"` as its **first** include.
- It must contain **no** other logic — its only purpose is to anchor the module's identity symbols via `MODULE_NAME_DECLARATION`.
- `MODULE_NAME_DECLARATION(BUILD_REFERENCE)` generates the module name string, SHA hash, and version identifiers that the Thunder framework uses to load and link the plugin shared library.

### Example

```cpp
#include "Module.h"

MODULE_NAME_DECLARATION(BUILD_REFERENCE)
```
